summary: DHH observes that Basecamp's director of engineering opening attracted a flood of applicants who didn't read the basic requirements or follow application instructions. He questions why people treat job applications like lottery tickets, speculating that disillusionment with AI-driven hiring processes has led candidates to stop putting in effort. He pushes back on this approach, noting that Basecamp has humans reviewing every application. He argues that spray-and-pray applications create a negative psychological feedback loop for the applicants themselves.
key_points: Most applicants to Basecamp openings don't meet basic requirements or follow application instructions
Candidates may assume AI screens applications, making effort feel pointless, but DHH is skeptical this is true for most roles
Basecamp reviews applications with humans who won't spend time on non-compliant submissions
Low-effort mass applications create a psychological toll through terrible callback ratios, worsening a negative feedback loop
Putting in genuine effort on fewer applications is a better strategy than spamming resumes
money_quotes: Virtually none of whom seem to have bothered reading the basic requirements for the opening or comply with the instructions on how to make a successful application.
Do people think this is a lottery?
Presumably this approach must work for some people, some of the time, right? Otherwise why would you keep operating like this?

There's a human on the other side, and that human is not going to spend five minutes pondering whether you might be a fit for the role, if you don't comply with the basic requirements, like including a cover letter.
I think there's a cost to that kind of job applications which show up in a psychological toll from a terrible applications-to-callbacks ratio.
I guess what I'm saying is make the effort or don't bother. We're not drawing names out of a hat here.
key_insight: Treating job applications as a numbers game hurts applicants more than it helps, creating a demoralizing feedback loop that rewards no one.
